option_label: one row per selectable + condition_set_id
option_description: Compile variant scopes into condition sets.
pros: Can reduce duplication and match rule-driven schemas.
implementation_notes: Do not synthesize until condition schema is stable.

option_label: hybrid variant matrix compiled into condition sets later
option_description: Keep variant rows now and compile later.
pros: Preserves evidence while allowing later optimization.
implementation_notes: Good bridge if schema is unsettled.

option_label: one row per selectable + variant_id
option_description: Represent availability as explicit variant-scoped rows.
cons: May be verbose; may not match final condition-set model.
implementation_notes: Preserve available/standard/not_available only.

option_label: proceed later with confident subset apply prototype
option_description: Future pass applies only approved confident subset tables after schema decisions.
cons: Still requires ID, UI, availability, and source-ref decisions first.
implementation_notes: No current apply is authorized.

option_label: defer apply until full schema/datapackage exists
option_description: Wait for schema and datapackage stability.
implementation_notes: Use if target tables remain incomplete.

option_label: produce reconciliation report against canonical CSV first
option_description: Compare confident subset to current canonical rows before any apply.
implementation_notes: Best next step if canonical data already has overlapping content.

option_label: keep only in generated proposal archive
option_description: Retain metadata in ignored/generated packet outputs only.
cons: Requires generated archive availability for future audit.
implementation_notes: Good if canonical governance is deferred.

option_label: preserve in change_log/provenance table
option_description: Map metadata to a future provenance or change log table.
implementation_notes: Do not implement until schema is explicit.

option_label: drop after apply
option_description: Do not carry proposal_status/review_status/filter metadata into canonical CSVs.
implementation_notes: Pair with generated archive retention.

option_label: preserve in import audit metadata
option_description: Store proposal metadata in audit/provenance outputs, not business tables.
implementation_notes: Conservative default for this pipeline.

option_label: map section_family to broad placeholder sections
option_description: Use importer section families as temporary broad UI sections.
pros: Fastest path to a prototype review.
cons: Not final UI taxonomy; can hide category nuance.
implementation_notes: Clearly mark as provisional if used.

option_label: defer finer UI mapping until app schema is stable
option_description: Keep section_family as proposal evidence only.
implementation_notes: Useful if schema-refactor is still moving.

option_label: use import map for section_family to section_id/step_id
option_description: Create explicit mapping config before apply.
implementation_notes: Preferred once target UI schema is stable.

option_label: manually assign IDs during apply
option_description: Human assigns canonical IDs during a future apply/reconciliation pass.
cons: Slower and harder to repeat automatically.
implementation_notes: Use for small reviewed slices or conflicts.

option_label: derive from model_key + rpo + disambiguator
option_description: Create canonical IDs from model scope, orderable RPO, and a deterministic disambiguator when needed.
pros: Readable and reviewable; keeps model-specific collisions visible.
cons: Needs collision policy and reconciliation against existing canonical IDs.
implementation_notes: Use only after a human-approved ID format.

option_label: use existing canonical IDs when reconciling against data/stingray
option_description: Match proposal evidence to existing IDs before minting new IDs.
implementation_notes: Best paired with a read-only canonical comparison pass.

option_label: derive from stable source hash
option_description: Use source evidence to create deterministic IDs.
pros: Stable across reruns if evidence is stable.
cons: Less readable and can churn if source references change.
implementation_notes: Hash inputs must be documented before use.

option_label: canonical meta/source_refs.csv row per source cell/field
option_description: Promote row-level source refs into canonical governance table.
implementation_notes: Use only after schema exists.

option_label: keep proposal source refs as import-only evidence
option_description: Archive generated source refs with proposals instead of canonical tables.
cons: Canonical rows lose direct row-level provenance unless copied elsewhere.
implementation_notes: Acceptable only if archive retention is approved.

option_label: source_ref member table linking canonical rows to source refs
option_description: Keep source refs normalized and link many refs to one canonical row.
cons: Requires an additional relation table decision.
implementation_notes: Good if multiple source cells support one canonical row.
